hypothesis: Per §16.1.2 factorial: does ordinal_ce_hybrid contribute beyond attention_pool?
lessons: Attention pool drives 76% of the combined gain; ordinal_hybrid contributes a small, just-significant 24%; no interaction.
Decision: promote attention pool alone. Carry ordinal_hybrid as a 'promising' branch for future campaigns.
Cost was 36.4 GPU-hours — twice a typical candidate. Factorials are expensive; consider whether the marginal information was worth it.

branch_insights.raw_observation: The factorial grid attributed most of the gain to attention_pool, while ordinal_hybrid contributed a small just-significant effect with no interaction.
branch_insights.distilled_insight: The next promotion path should prioritize attention_pool alone and carry ordinal_hybrid as a lower-priority future branch.
branch_insights.validated_constraint: Plan the attention_pool lesion and promotion request before spending more budget on ordinal_hybrid combinations.
branch_insights.retirement_signal: Revisit ordinal_hybrid priority after a fresh split or a cheaper targeted calibration experiment.

next_actions: Plan §16.1.1 lesion test on attention_pool (attention weights → uniform)
Emit promotion request for attention_pool alone
